subject: Update: Back pay for low- and mid-wage federal contract workers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
I need you to stand with me in righting this wrong.

Unlike the 800,000 furloughed federal employees, these workers didn't receive back pay when the government reopened. **And now, with the government only funded through Friday, these contract workers are at risk of losing out on pay once again.** **My bill to ensure contract workers get the back pay they deserve is gaining bipartisan support, but I need you to help us get this over the finish line.[We have no time to waste: With the government funded only through Friday, I'm asking you to add your name today to keep up the fight for contract workers.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d0dd/c1f7015/614530d9/1140eaec/233106160/VEsC/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/rtw_201902_backpay-ld?source=rtw_20190212_em-ld")** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d0dd/c1f7015/614530d9/1140eaec/233106160/VEsD/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/rtw_201902_backpay-ld?source=rtw_20190212_em-ld") Let me be clear: If we don't reimburse these people for the wages they lose, they'll be forced to make choices between paying for vital prescriptions and putting food on the table. This is deeply unfair, and I need you to stand with me in righting this wrong. **[If you agree it's wrong that hardworking people didn't get the pay they deserve because of Trump's shutdown, make your voice heard by adding your name to my petition.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d0dd/c1f7015/614530d9/1140eaec/233106160/VEsB/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/rtw_201902_backpay-ld?source=rtw_20190212_em-ld")** Let's do this, Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. subject: No more paying for the rich world’s medicine
body:
After today’s Cabinet meeting, Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar took a moment to explain how America’s seniors... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3455e1882c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • February 12, 2019
---
| | |
# **No more paying for the rich world’s medicine**

**After today’s Cabinet meeting** , Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar took a moment to explain how America’s seniors are footing the drug bills for patients in some of the richest countries in the world.

**“So under President Trump** , what we’ve found is that in our Medicare program for senior citizens, for the drugs that doctors administer in their offices to patients . . . we’re paying 180 percent of what the Europeans, the Canadians, and the Japanese are paying for the exact same drugs,” Secretary Azar says. “Pharma is making all their profit here in the United States, and then they’re giving these sweetheart deals in the other countries.”

**Putting American patients first** has been core to President Trump’s agenda since day one. Last week, during his second State of the Union address, he called on Congress to join him: “The next major priority for me, and for all of us, should be to lower the cost of healthcare and prescription drugs, and to protect patients with preexisting conditions.”

**Already** , **President Trump’s** efforts to put patients in control have resulted in the single largest decline in drug prices in 46 years. The FDA approved a record number of generic drugs in 2017 and 2018 as alternatives to expensive brand-name medicines. All told, these approvals have saved customers a staggering $26 billion.

**The next chapter** in the fight against high drug prices is increasing transparency and ending the practice of global free-riding. Hospitals, insurance companies, and drug companies should be required to disclose real prices to patients to drive costs down. And Americans must get a share of whatever discounts other wealthy countries receive.

**“This is wrong** , this is unfair, and together we will stop it,” President Trump said last Tuesday. “And we’ll stop it fast.”

[Watch: It’s time to stop paying for the rich world’s medicine.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bbcd3c4166&e=3ee4985eea)

**Read:** [“How Team Trump is bringing drug prices down”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e91b0b40ef&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump: Every option is on the table to make sure the wall is built**

**“I’ll have to study it,”** President Trump said today about an emerging proposal in Congress to address border security funding. But one thing remains nonnegotiable: “It’s very simple. We’re building a wall.”

**Experts on the ground** have made it clear that physical barriers are needed, and progress is being made. “We just started a big, big section on the Rio Grande. You probably saw it,” the President told reporters today. “I never kid about construction.”

**The President kicked off** today’s Cabinet meeting by reminding Americans of his message in last week’s State of the Union address. “I am eager to work with both parties to deliver for all Americans,” he said—and that means for immigration reform and beyond.

[Watch President Trump open today’s Cabinet meeting.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=694ef80536&e=3ee4985eea)

**More** : [Democrats want to cut ICE. “We are not going to let that happen.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dafa6ec106&e=3ee4985eea)

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Job Openings Hit Record High of 7.3 Million in December
body:
"In the past three months, the economy has added an average of 241,000 net new jobs a month." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7d2f4d5e0a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Job Openings Hit Record High of 7.3 Million in December**

“The number of job openings hit a record high of 7.3 million in December, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported Tuesday in a sign of the labor market’s strength,” Joseph Lawler reports in the _Washington Examiner_.

“The construction industry recorded 382,000 new posts, far more than ever before . . . In the past three months, the economy has added an average of 241,000 net new jobs a month, more than twice as much as needed to keep up with population growth and keep unemployment trending down.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6e0a64e80e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “USA Today ran a surprising story on Tuesday headlined ‘Can the middle-class revival under Trump last?’ It's surprising — shocking really — because it might just be the first time a major news outlet has admitted that there has been a middle-class revival under Trump,” the _[Investor’s Business Daily](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5acd48cef0&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “It's a safe bet that had a Democrat been in the White House over the past two years things would be different.”
---
|
---
| “U.S. manufacturing is once again in the news. New data shows America’s factories adding 261,000 jobs from January 2018 to January 2019. In the State of the Union, President Trump emphasized the urgency of confronting predatory trade from countries like China,” Michael Stumo writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d2aafdcdc9&e=3ee4985eea)_. “There’s no doubt that America’s manufacturers are currently rebounding. The tariffs that President Trump imposed a year ago on steel, aluminum, solar panels and washing machines have already created more than 11,000 new jobs.”
---
|
---
| “The Donald Trump White House is more than capable of doing conventional things well. Last week’s State of the Union saw a complex policy process synthesized into a well-delivered prime-time address that captured the president’s unique policy agenda and won high praise,” Raj Shah writes in _[USA Today](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=78e617529b&e=3ee4985eea)_. “But if conventional management styles alone could make or break a presidency, historians would be praising Jimmy Carter and vilifying Ronald Reagan.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0ea52aefad&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c1397c1a92&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=46b9175a2a)
---

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Both sides agree on this:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter, The same day it was announced that I'd been added to the DCCC's Frontline Program, the National Republican Campaign Committee put me at the top of their target list for 2020. Top strategists on both sides of the aisle are now predicting our race is going to be highly competitive in 2020.
| | Hi Peter,

As one of our top supporters, I want to make sure you saw Congresswoman Bustos's note below.

The same day it was announced that I'd been added to the DCCC's Frontline Program, the National Republican Campaign Committee put me at the top of their target list for 2020. **Top strategists on both sides of the aisle are now predicting our race is going to be highly competitive in 2020.**

We have a strategy to dissuade potential challengers and maintain a position of strength in this swing district, but it hinges on [**your ongoing support**](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/2gzETBtJ0oal3L88bs1VTl3vDRs9sRcj6SSh5m93KZU=?refcode=em_fr_2019_02.13_b1_as_all&amount=25). You see, if we don't show that the grassroots movement we built last year is still as strong as ever, it will signal to special interests that our district is a strategic place to funnel their millions. We can't risk that, Peter.

**The stronger we start this next campaign, the easier the road ahead will be.[That's why I'm asking for your support today.](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/2gzETBtJ0oal3L88bs1VTl3vDRs9sRcj6SSh5m93KZU=?refcode=em_fr_2019_02.13_b1_as_all&amount=25)**

In Congress, I'm focused on laying out my priorities for Central Virginia that include investing in broadband, bringing down the cost of prescriptions drugs, and curbing the influence of big money in our politics. While I work to get results for our community, I so appreciate [**your help**](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/2gzETBtJ0oal3L88bs1VTl3vDRs9sRcj6SSh5m93KZU=?refcode=em_fr_2019_02.13_b1_as_all&amount=25) in keeping our campaign going full speed ahead.

\-- Abigail

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Cheri Bustos**
Date: Mon, Feb 11th, 2019 at 2:06 PM
Subject: I have Abigail's back, do you?

Friends,

I wanted you to hear the news from me first: I just added Abigail Spanberger to the DCCC's Frontline Program -- our list of vital Democratic incumbents who are critical in our mission to protect our Majority in 2020.

I need to be frank in this really big moment: [**Will you help Abigail build on this incredible momentum by chipping in a grassroots donation to her campaign today?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/2gzETBtJ0oal3L88bs1VTl3vDRs9sRcj6SSh5m93KZU=?refcode=em_fr_2019_02.13_b1_as_all&amount=25)

After our historic election in 2018, we gained an incredible group of Democrats, like Abigail, who entered the halls of Congress with one mission in mind: **to help improve the lives of the people she represents in Virginia.**

Folks, Abigail has been a fearless advocate in our fight for health care, for accountability, and for the protection of every American's right to use their voice at the ballot box. And truthfully, it's up to us to make sure Abigail remains a powerful voice for each and every one of us in our nation's capital.

But Abigail's path to victory won't be easy. She's up against powerful special interests, who are prepared to do (_and spend_) whatever it takes to rip this Democratic seat from all of us who worked so hard to elect Abigail.

**We need to show them that we have Abigail 's back -- and are ready to step up to fight for someone who works tirelessly to promote our Democratic priorities.**

[**So, friends, can Abigail and I count on your support today?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/2gzETBtJ0oal3L88bs1VTl3vDRs9sRcj6SSh5m93KZU=?refcode=em_fr_2019_02.13_b1_as_all&amount=25)

Thanks for all you do,

Cheri Bustos
Chairwoman, DCCC

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/2gzETBtJ0oal3L88bs1VTl3vDRs9sRcj6SSh5m93KZU=?refcode=em_fr_2019_02.13_b1_as_all&amount=25)

_Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/wgYI0XUkiXrwSzGMBAZfeLIDMTqkUjBv1ahfT6K934E=/fsK_rKpQ-TvfBVRgpQtGXMA6CuGCxT7dRxbJtGxN8Ww=)_
| Paid for by Spanberger for Congress

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Not one more person should suffer under socialism
body:
Today, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump welcomed President Ivan Duque Marquez and First Lady Maria Juliana Ruiz Sandoval.

https://www.whitehouse.gov/1600daily/
The White House • February 13, 2019

** Not one more person should suffer under socialism
------------------------------------------------------------
Today, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump welcomed President Ivan Duque Marquez and First Lady Maria Juliana Ruiz Sandoval of Colombia to the White House. “We’re working on many things together: the eradication of drugs in Colombia and outside of Colombia; and obviously, Venezuela,” President Trump said.

“We want to work together to put an end to the brutal dictatorship that has been affecting the Venezuelan people,” President Duque added.

Three weeks ago, the United States officially recognized Juan Guaido as the legitimate president of Venezuela. “We stand with the Venezuelan people in their noble quest for freedom, and we condemn the brutality of the Maduro regime, whose socialist policies have turned that nation from being the wealthiest in South America into a state of abject poverty and despair,” President Trump said in his State of the Union address last week.

To keep its word, America has imposed tough sanctions on Maduro and his corrupt associates. The Trump Administration is blocking assets in the United States controlled by Venezuela’s state-owned oil company, disconnecting Maduro and his cronies from their needed revenue sources and protecting crucial assets for the future of Venezuela.

Here at home, mainstream Americans are alarmed by new calls to adopt socialism in our own country. From Venezuela to the historic horrors of Maoist China, Cuba, and the Soviet Union, socialism turns modern prosperity into primitive scarcity. Even Europe’s Nordic countries—often held up by American liberals as socialist “success stories”—reveal the failure of state control: Today, living standards in the Nordic countries are at least 15 percent lower than in the United States, according to the Council of Economic Advisers.

“America was founded on liberty and independence, and not government coercion, domination, and control,” President Trump told Congress last week. “Tonight, we renew our resolve that America will never be a socialist country.”

President Trump is standing up for the people of Venezuela (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-supports-venezuelan-peoples-efforts-restore-democracy-country/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) .

White House report: The true costs of socialism (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/cea-report-opportunity-costs-socialism/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

** Top CEOs, including Apple’s Tim Cook, team up with Ivanka Trump
------------------------------------------------------------
In the world's best economy, a skills gap shouldn't stand between hardworking Americans and 7.3 million available jobs. So 25 leaders—including Fortune 500 CEOs, nonprofit executives, and governors—are joining Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump and Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross to get more Americans into the jobs they want.

The American Workforce Policy Advisory Board, announced by Secretary Ross and Ms. Trump today, will work with President Trump’s National Council for the American Worker to help our labor force navigate the technological disruptions and rapidly changing nature of work today.

The board’s goal is to “ensure inclusive growth” in the U.S. economy, Ms. Trump says. Joining the board will be Apple CEO Tim Cook, Walmart CEO Doug McMillon, and 23 other leaders from across business, the nonprofit sector, and government.

Bloomberg: ‘Wilbur Ross, Ivanka Trump introduce workforce advisory board’ (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/icymi-bloomberg-wilbur-ross-ivanka-trump-introduce-workforce-advisory-board/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

The pledge: More than 200 companies have pledged opportunities for workers (https://www.whitehouse.gov/pledge-to-americas-workers/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump meet with Colombian President Ivan Duque Marquez and his wife Mrs. Maria Juliana Ruiz Sandoval | February 13, 2019

subject: So Just How Radical Is This New Democratic Party?
body:
So Just How Radical Is This New Democratic Party? No Images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-jy/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-y/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-j/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-t/)
### [So Just How Radical Is This New Democratic Party?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-i/)
Pretty darn radical. John F. Kennedy sure wouldn't recognize it. He believed instinctively in a strong America. In lower taxes to spur economic growth. In a robust foreign policy, and an adequate military to support it. In less government, not more. Remember "ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country?" [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Are We Headed for Another Shutdown?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-u/)
Well the longest government shutdown in U.S. history is finally over. Of course we could be right back in the middle of another one in less than three weeks. So what's going to happen? [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [What the heck is a "Green New Deal"?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-p/)
Well, the partial government shutdown drags on. Today is day 33 - the longest shutdown in U.S. history. We've seen Speaker Pelosi uninvite President Trump to the State of the Union. We've seen President Trump counterpunch, and cancel Pelosi's plane ride to the Middle East. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-x/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-c/)
### [Hold on to Your Wallet](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthrkikk-kujkulhluu-q/)
There are a whole bunch of Democrats who would like to be "the chosen one" to run against President Trump in the next election.
